response:
Based on the provided context, the **Ckm wz. 25 Hotchkiss** was a Polish derivative of the **Hotchkiss M1914 machine gun**.

The text explicitly states that the Ckm wz. 25 Hotchkiss was a "Polish derivative of the Hotchkiss M1914 machine gun." It further confirms that the **Hotchkiss M1914** "became the standard machine gun of the French Army during World War I."
